button.title: Button
button.intent: The Button Widget allows designers to create customizable, clickable buttons without requiring additional plugins or shortcodes. This versatile element functions as a core interaction component for directing user actions toward specific destinations or triggering defined behaviors on web pages.
use_cases: Adding call-to-action elements that guide visitors toward conversions
Creating navigation links with enhanced visual styling and interactivity
Building form submission triggers or download prompts
Designing multi-button layouts for product selections or feature highlights
Establishing consistent clickable elements throughout page designs
All available widgets are displayed
Click or drag the widget to the canvas
For more information, see Add elements to a page

Button text customization with full typography controls (font, size, weight, color)
Background color, gradient, and hover state styling options
Padding and margin adjustments for spacing control

Icon placement options (before/after text or standalone)
Size presets (small, medium, large) or custom dimensions
Advanced styling including shadow, opacity, and transform effects

code-highlight.title: Code Highlight
code-highlight.intent: The Code Highlight widget displays formatted code snippets with syntax highlighting for better readability. It is designed to showcase code samples on web pages with proper formatting and visual distinction of different code elements, making it ideal for developer documentation or technical blogs.
use_cases: Displaying code examples or tutorials on your website
Creating documentation pages that reference code snippets
Teaching programming concepts with visual code samples
Building developer resource pages or technical blogs
Showcasing API examples or configuration code
Organizing your layout design and structuring content elements inside Elementor.
Enhancing user experience by presenting information in a clean, professional, and accessible layout.
Customizing specific styles, responsiveness, and display logic for elements across devices.
settings_highlights: Language selection for syntax highlighting (multiple languages supported)
Code input area for pasting or writing code
Theme options for code appearance
Line numbers toggle
Copy-to-clipboard functionality
Font size and family customization
Background color options
Border and padding controls

Language – Select your coding language to properly highlight your code’s syntax. Options include 50+ of supported languages such as Markup, HTML, XML, SVG, Mathml, CSS, Javascript, ActionScript, PHP, Ruby, etc
Code – Enter the snippet of code that you wish to display on the page in the field. This may also use Dynamic Tag data from ACF or other plugins
Line Numbers – Choose Show to display line numbers next to each line
Copy to Clipboard – Choose Show to allow users to copy the code with a single click on the Copy button in the upper right corner of the code box

Highlight Lines – Designate one or more lines to be highlighted by entering a value or using a Dynamic Tag. Sets of numbers should be separated by a comma. (e.g. 2-7, 10, 13-15 would highlight lines 2 through 7, line 10, and lines 13 through 15.)
Word Wrap – Toggle to Show/Hide word wrapping
Theme – Select a theme for the code display. Both light and dark themes are available to choose from
Height – Enter a height for the code box
limits: Very long code blocks may require scrolling on mobile devices
Some programming languages may have limited syntax highlighting support
Performance may be affected with multiple large code blocks on one page
The widget requires proper code formatting for optimal display

Presenting service offerings with icons/images and detailed descriptions
Creating portfolio galleries where hover reveals project details

Designing testimonial cards that flip to show full customer feedback
The Flip Box Widget helps you create animated boxes that flip to the other side, when a visitor hovers over them

global.title: Global
global.intent: Global widgets allow you to create reusable widget instances that can be placed across multiple pages and posts. When you update a global widget, all instances update simultaneously, ensuring consistency and reducing manual editing work across your site.
use_cases: You need the same widget or content block appearing on multiple pages
You want to maintain consistent messaging across your site
You need to make updates in one place that reflect everywhere
You're building headers, footers, or sidebar content
You want to streamline your workflow for frequently-used components
Create a global widget
When you create a global widget, it is added to the list of templates
As a template it is assigned a shortcode and can be added to a page as a shortcode
For information about using shortcodes, see Elementor Shortcodes
settings_highlights: Save any existing widget as a global widget via context menu
Access global widgets from the widgets panel for easy insertion
Edit global widget definition to update all instances site-wide
Create new global widgets from scratch in the library
Convert global widgets back to regular widgets when needed
Organize and manage all global widgets from a centralized location
Link multiple page instances to a single global widget definition
Content options – Configure general content, title, tags, and icons.
Style settings – Customize colors, borders, background, padding, and typography.
Advanced features – Apply custom CSS classes, ID, and responsiveness properties.
limits: Global widget updates apply universally — be cautious with broad changes
Some dynamic content may behave differently in global context

star-rating.title: Star Rating
star-rating.intent: Display beautiful, customizable star ratings on WordPress sites using CSS styling. The widget generates rich snippets for search engine optimization, improves organic visibility, and enhances user trust through visual product/service ratings. It supports dynamic values pulled from custom fields, making it suitable for programmatic rating displays without hardcoding values.
use_cases: Showcasing product or service ratings directly on a page or landing section
Improving search engine rankings with schema markup (rich snippets) for star ratings
Building review sections that require visual star displays alongside testimonial text
Pulling dynamic rating values from ACF or other custom field data automatically
Adding credibility signals to landing pages or product detail pages
settings_highlights: **Icon library**: Choose between Font Awesome 5 or Unicode star characters as the icon source
**Rating value**: Set the numeric rating (e.g. 4.5 out of 5); accepts decimal values
**Star count**: Configure total number of stars displayed (scale denominator)
**Size**: Adjust star icon dimensions to fit heading, body, or compact contexts
**Color (filled)**: Brand-matched fill color for active/rated stars
**Color (empty)**: Muted or outline color for unrated star slots
**Spacing**: Control gap between individual star icons
**Alignment**: Left / center / right alignment within the container
**Dynamic tags**: Bind rating value to a custom field for programmatic display
**Rich snippets toggle**: Emit schema.org `AggregateRating` markup for SEO
limits: Rich snippets require proper schema implementation; incomplete setup may not improve search visibility
Dynamic tag functionality depends on having properly configured custom fields with numeric rating data
Star styling limited to Font Awesome 5 or Unicode options; custom SVG icons require CSS overrides or workarounds
Rating values must be numeric structured data; free-text ratings ("Excellent") will not display as visual stars

**Heading tags**: Choose which H-tag levels (H2–H6) are included in the TOC

toggle.title: Toggle
toggle.intent: If you are using nested elements, the Accordion widget (Accordion with nested elements) incorporates the capabilities of Toggle widget and replaces it. This means the Toggle widget will no longer be available. Existing toggles are unaffected by this change and they can still be edited.
use_cases: When a page is loaded, all Toggle widget items are collapsed
With the Accordion widget, however, the first item is expanded, while all other items remain collapsed
With the Toggle widget, visitors can expand as many items as they like
With the Accordion widget they are limited to only expanding one item at a time
